mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. It provides a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Install the Langflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow CLI tool which is necessary for managing your Langflow deployments.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Install the specific version of Langflow.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Google Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This ensures your kubectl commands target the correct cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Langflow to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Install Langflow in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Langflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace langflow

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml --namespace langflow

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Langflow deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This removes the Langflow release.
> This deletes the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods --namespace langflow

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): deployments.apps "langflow" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Langflow deployment may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm installation logs and ensure the namespace is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "langflow:latest"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the application configuration or dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.7.0, ensure to update your API calls to the new endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
